fixes.title: docker-compose.yml + drift_exceptions.json bootstrap + drift_decision.rego comment
fixes.description: Added drift_exceptions.json volume mount to opa-server service. Created bootstrap .cloudsentinel/drift_exceptions.json with empty exceptions list so OPA server starts without errors. Replaced incorrect comment about OPA recursion with accurate architectural explanation.

fixes.title: drift_decision.rego — Replace deprecated future.keywords imports with rego.v1
fixes.description: Replaced 'import future.keywords.if' and 'import future.keywords.in' with 'import rego.v1' — the canonical import for OPA >= 0.59. The future.keywords form is deprecated and will be removed in a future OPA release.

fixes.title: Severity casing inconsistency across drift engine components
fixes.description: OPA returns ALL CAPS severities (CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, INFO) but DefectDojo and the drift engine use Title case (Critical, High, Medium, Low, Info). Added _OPA_SEVERITY_NORMALIZE dict in enrichment.py to normalize on ingestion. Fixed json_normalizer.py to use OPA severity when opa_evaluated=True. Changed _OCSF_ORDER from 'Informational' to 'Info' in drift-engine.py and updated default severity accordingly.

fixes.title: drift_decision.rego missing environment scope check for exceptions
fixes.description: Drift exceptions were not validated against the current environment (production/staging/dev). Added _drift_exception_env_matches() helper rules with empty-list-means-universal semantics, and added the check to valid_drift_exception(). Added 'environment' field to opa_normalizer.normalize_drift_for_opa() output using DRIFT_ENVIRONMENT or CI_ENVIRONMENT_NAME env vars, defaulting to 'production'. Added 'import os' to opa_normalizer.py.

fixes.title: Incorrect comment about OPA recursion in drift_decision.rego
fixes.description: The comment incorrectly stated the _drift_exceptions_store assignment needed to reference a specific path 'to avoid OPA recursion'. OPA does not recurse on data document references. Replaced with accurate explanation: data.cloudsentinel.drift_exceptions is a static data document loaded at OPA server startup via --data flag, architecturally separate from the Rego package being evaluated.

fixes.title: Missing approved_at < expires_at temporal coherence check in valid_drift_exception()
fixes.description: valid_drift_exception() checked that approved_at <= now and now < expires_at, but did not verify that approved_at < expires_at. A malformed exception with expires_at before approved_at would pass if now happened to be in neither range. Added the temporal coherence check time.parse_rfc3339_ns(ex.approved_at) < time.parse_rfc3339_ns(ex.expires_at).

fixes.title: Create fetch_drift_exceptions.py for shift-right exception governance
fixes.description: Created shift-right/scripts/fetch_drift_exceptions.py that fetches Risk Acceptances tagged 'cloudsentinel-drift' from DefectDojo and transforms them into drift_exceptions.json for OPA. Supports scope binding (repo/branch/environment), deterministic SHA-256 IDs for stable exception correlation, and dry-run mode. Added 'fetch-drift-exceptions' Makefile target.

fixes.title: resource_id ambiguity — ARM ID vs Terraform address
fixes.description: opa_normalizer was passing item['resource_id'] (Azure ARM ID, may be None for undeployed resources) to OPA as resource_id, while drift_decision.rego used finding.address for exception matching. Changed opa_normalizer to use item['address'] (Terraform resource address, always present and stable) as resource_id. Added defensive assertion warning in enrichment.py for violations missing resource_id. Added clarifying comment in drift_decision.rego evaluate_drift().

fixes.title: Add separate OPA server for shift-right (port 8182) in docker-compose.yml
fixes.description: The single opa-server handled both shift-left (pipeline_decision.rego) and shift-right (drift_decision.rego) policies on port 8181, violating architectural separation. Added new 'opa-server-shiftright' service on port 8182 loading only drift_decision.rego and drift_exceptions.json. Removed drift_decision.rego from opa-server (now shift-left only). Updated drift_config.yaml OPA server_url default from port 8181 to 8182.

fixes.title: Wildcard check missing in drift exception validation
fixes.description: valid_drift_exception() did not reject exceptions containing wildcards (* or ?) in resource_type or resource_id fields. A wildcard exception like resource_type='azurerm_*' would match all Azure resource types, creating an overly broad exception that masks real violations. Added _drift_exception_has_wildcard() helper rules and not _drift_exception_has_wildcard(ex) condition in valid_drift_exception().

tests_added.import_fix: Updated 'import future.keywords.*' to 'import rego.v1' in test file

remaining_known_issues: NSG SSH source_address_prefix = '*' bug in infra/azure/student-secure/network/main.tf (B0 from CLAUDE.md audit — out of scope for this fix batch)
Trivy image scan hardcoded to alpine:3.21 (separate infrastructure concern)
APPSEC_ALLOWED_USERS contains personal account drghassen (governance concern)
